ops.title: Never leave comments explaining that a construct exists for a tool's benefit (great-docs, linters, CI) — the construct stands alone
ops.body:
User called comments like `# great-docs documents __all__ when present; keep it in sync…` "trash comments" and asked to "clean up all that kinda thing" (2026-07-02, cc-pushback).

**Why:** A comment justifying code to a tool or process is noise — if the construct (an `__all__`, a pin, a config knob) is needed, it needs no advocate; if its reason must live somewhere, that's a memory/docs concern, not inline prose. This is stricter than STYLEGUIDE.md's "non-obvious workarounds" exception — tooling breadcrumbs don't qualify.

**How to apply:** When adding a workaround for docs builds, type checkers, or CI, add the construct bare. Delete existing comments of this genre on sight when touching a file. Test comments explaining non-obvious *test intent* (why a fixture is shaped oddly, what an assertion pins) are a different genre and stay. See [[cc-pushback-greatdocs-all-gotcha]] for the underlying great-docs fact — the knowledge lives there, not in the source.
